full_response:
We work with the usual ( −,+,+,+ ) Minkowski signature, so that  
g_{ab}U^{a}U^{b}=−1 for a timelike 4–velocity and g_{ab}S^{a}S^{b}>0 for a spacelike vector.

1.  Review: projection orthogonal to a timelike unit vector U⁽ᵃ⁾  
 P_{ab}(U)=g_{ab}+U_{a}U_{b}.  
 Because U^{b}U_{b}=−1,  
  P_{ab}U^{b}=U_{a}+U_{a}(−1)=0,   
so P_{ab} indeed “kills’’ the component along U^{a} and projects into the 3–space orthogonal to U^{a}.

2.  Generalization to an arbitrary spacelike vector  
Let S^{a} be any spacelike 4–vector, not necessarily unit, so that  

  g_{ab}S^{a}S^{b}\equiv S^{2}>0 .  

Define the (unit) spacelike direction  
  n^{a}=S^{a}/√S^{2},  so that  g_{ab}n^{a}n^{b}=+1.

3.  Candidate projector  
Guided by the timelike case, reverse the sign in front of the dyad:

  P_{ab}(S)=g_{ab}-n_{a}n_{b}
      =g_{ab}-\dfrac{S_{a}S_{b}}{S^{2}}.     (1)

4.  Verification  
a)  Orthogonality:  

 P_{ab}(S)S^{b} = g_{ab}S^{b} - n_{a}(n_{b}S^{b})  
        = S_{a} - n_{a}(+1)√S^{2}  
        = S_{a} - S_{a}=0.  

Thus P_{ab}(S) annihilates S^{a}; anything it outputs is orthogonal to S^{a}.

b)  Idempotence:  

 P^{a}{}_{c}P^{c}{}_{b}
  = (δ^{a}_{c}-n^{a}n_{c})(δ^{c}_{b}-n^{c}n_{b})
  = δ^{a}_{b}-n^{a}n_{b}-n^{a}n_{b}+n^{a}n_{c}n^{c}n_{b}  
  = δ^{a}_{b}-n^{a}n_{b}=P^{a}{}_{b},

because n^{c}n_{c}=+1.  Hence P_{ab}(S) is a true projection operator.

c)  Trace: in four‐dimensional spacetime, tr P = g^{ab}P_{ab}=4−1=3, as expected for projection onto a three‐dimensional subspace.

5.  Result  
Equation (1) is therefore the desired projector onto the 3–space orthogonal to an arbitrary spacelike vector.

\boxed{\,P_{ab}(S)=g_{ab}-\dfrac{S_{a}S_{b}}{g_{cd}S^{c}S^{d}}\,}

(If S^{a} is already unit-normalised, this simplifies to P_{ab}=g_{ab}-S_{a}S_{b}.)

Confidence: 89%
